description:
This diagram illustrates a data processing workflow for generating L2b and L3 products from L2a products and Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) model data, likely within the context of European Ground Motion Service (EGMS) Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) data calibration.

The workflow begins with a `GNSS Model` which branches into two parallel processes:
1.  `GNSS Model Interpolation`.
2.  `GNSS N/S Component in SAR LOS` (Synthetic Aperture Radar Line Of Sight).

The `GNSS Model Interpolation` output, along with `L2a Products`, feeds into `Interpolated Model Projection`. The output of `Interpolated Model Projection` then proceeds to `SAR/GNSS Referencing`. `SAR/GNSS Referencing` leads to a `Compensation` step, which also receives direct input from `L2a Products`. This first `Compensation` step produces `L2b Products`.

The `L2b Products` then feed into a second `Compensation` step. This second `Compensation` step also receives input from the `GNSS N/S Component in SAR LOS` branch. The output of the second `Compensation` step feeds into `Decomposition`. Finally, `Decomposition` produces `L3 Products`, representing the end product of this processing chain.
